prerequisites: No credentials, API key, or external account are required. This source generates sample data locally using the Python Faker library and does not connect to any external API.
An Airbyte workspace (or compatible runtime) in which to configure the Sample Data (Faker) source.

authMethods.name: No authentication required
authMethods.summary: This connector has no secret fields and does not authenticate against any service. It generates data locally, so there is nothing to authorize. Configuration is limited to the data-generation parameters described below.

setupSteps.title: Add the Sample Data (Faker) source
setupSteps.body: Create a new source in your Airbyte workspace and select Sample Data (Faker). No credentials are requested because the connector produces records locally rather than calling an external API.

setupSteps.title: Set the record volume
setupSteps.body: Use the 'count' field to control the total number of user records to generate. It is optional and defaults to 1000; the purchases stream scales proportionally to this value.

setupSteps.title: Make output deterministic (optional)
setupSteps.body: Set the 'seed' field to a specific integer to produce the same records on every sync. It is optional and defaults to -1, which produces randomized data each run.

setupSteps.title: Tune checkpointing and parallelism (optional)
setupSteps.body: Set 'records_per_slice' to control how many records are emitted per stream slice before a state checkpoint (default 1000), and set 'parallelism' to the number of parallel workers used for generation (default 4, set to the number of CPUs allocated to the connector).

setupSteps.title: Control resync behavior (optional)
setupSteps.body: Enable 'always_updated' so every sync re-emits all records with fresh updated_at timestamps (default enabled). Disable it to stop emitting after the initial sync.
